மிருகம் கொன்று

மிருகம் மட்டும் வளர்கிறதே

நந்தகுமாரா
நந்தகுமாரா
நாளை மிருகம்
கொல்வாயா

மிருகம் கொன்ற
எச்சம் கொண்டு
மீண்டும் கடவுள் செய்வாயா

குரங்கில் இருந்து
மனிதன் என்றால்
மனிதன் நிறையாய்

பிறந்த மனிதா
தெய்வ ஜோதியில்
கலப்பாயா
நந்தகுமாரா

கலந்து செய்த கலவை நான்

காற்றில் ஏறி
மலையில் ஆடி
கவிதை பாடும்
பறவை நான்

ஒவ்வொரு துளியும்
ஒவ்வொரு துளியும்
உயிரில் வேர்கள்
குளிர்கிறதே

எல்லாம் துளியும்
குளிரும்போது
இரு துளி மட்டும்
சுடுகிறதே

நந்தகுமாரா
நந்தகுமாரா
மழை நீர் சுடாது
தெரியாதா

கன்னம் வழிகிற
கண்ணீர் துளிதான்

அறிவாயா

சுட்ட மழையும்
சுடாத மழையும்

seo.title: Kadavul Paadhi Lyrics Aalavandhan
seo.description: Full Kadavul Paadhi songs lyrics, Aalavandhan Songs Lyrics. sharable lyrics snippets, Lyrics: Vairamuthu, Music: Shankar-Eshaan-Loy, Singer(s): Kamal Haasan, Nandhini.
seo.keywords: Vairamuthu, Aalavandhan, Shankar-Eshaan-Loy, Kamal Haasan, Nandhini, Kadavul Paadhi full lyrics, Aalavandhan songs lyrics, Kadavul Paadhi lyrics snippets, Kadavul Paadhi WhatsApp status lyrics snippets, share Kadavul Paadhi lyrics snippets with friends, Tamil lyrics, Tamil songs

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kadavul Paadhi?

The song explores the duality of human nature, reflecting on the balance between divine and animalistic instincts.

Who are the singers of this song?

Kadavul Paadhi is sung by Kamal Haasan and Nandhini.

What role does this song play in the movie Aalavandhan?

The song underscores the protagonist's internal conflict and philosophical musings.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Kadavul Paadhi was composed by the trio Shankar-Eshaan-Loy.

When was the song released?

The song was released in 2001 as part of the movie Aalavandhan.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Kadavul Paadhi is a profound exploration of the duality within human nature, set against the backdrop of the movie Aalavandhan. The song's lyrics, penned by Vairamuthu, delve into the philosophical themes of divinity and animal instincts coexisting within a person. Kamal Haasan's and Nandhini's vocals bring a haunting depth to the composition by Shankar-Eshaan-Loy. This track evokes introspection and a sense of melancholy, perfectly aligning with the film's narrative of internal conflict and self-discovery.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Kadavul Paadhi from the movie Aalavandhan is a hauntingly introspective track that delves into the duality of human nature. With Kamal Haasan's evocative vocals and the profound lyrics by Vairamuthu, this song stands out as a unique collaboration with the musical trio Shankar-Eshaan-Loy. Experience the blend of philosophical depth and musical brilliance that makes this song a timeless piece.
stanzaMeanings: I am a mix made of half god and half beast.
Outside a beast, inside a god, I am a poem that cannot be understood.
I watch as beasts kill beasts, and god nurtures them.
But when god kills and eats, only the beast grows.
Nandhakumara, Nandhakumara, will you kill the beast tomorrow and make god again from the remains?
If a man evolved from monkeys, will he count himself as human? Born in the animal kingdom, will you mix with divine light?
Nandhakumara...
I am a mix made of half god and half beast.
Outside a beast, inside a god, I am a poem that cannot be understood.
I watch as beasts kill beasts, and god nurtures them, but when god kills and eats, only the beast grows.
Nandha
I am a mix made of half god and half beast.
I am a bird that climbs the wind, dances on mountains, and sings poetry.
I am a mix made of half god and half beast.
I am a bird that climbs the wind, dances on mountains, and sings poetry.
Every drop, every drop, the roots in life are shivering.
When all drops are cold, only two drops are burning.
Nandhakumara, Nandhakumara, can't you tell the difference between hot and cold rainwater?
Do you know the tear drop running down the cheek is like a drop of white water?
Aren't you the one who saw both the burning and non-burning rain as one?
Aren't you the one who made me bathe in both tear rain and water rain?
